name: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center
description: Iran's primary nuclear research and uranium conversion complex, 15 km southeast of Isfahan. Hosts a Uranium Conversion Facility that converts yellowcake into UF6 feedstock for enrichment centrifuges. Under IAEA safeguards since capabilities were publicly revealed in 2002.

name: Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility
hardening: UNDERGROUND — main FEP halls buried 8m underground beneath reinforced concrete and earth berm
description: Iran's largest declared uranium enrichment facility, 225 km south of Tehran. Underground halls house thousands of gas centrifuges enriching UF6. Target of Stuxnet cyberattack (2010) and sabotage explosion (2020). Central to international negotiations over Iran's nuclear program.

name: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
hardening: MOUNTAIN — built deep inside a mountain near Qom, under ~80m of rock, designed to withstand aerial bombardment
description: Heavily fortified underground enrichment plant built into a mountain near Qom, 150 km south of Tehran. Revealed by Western intelligence in 2009. Iran has enriched uranium up to 60% purity here — one of the most strategically significant sites in Iran's nuclear program.

name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
description: Iran's only operational civilian nuclear power station on the Persian Gulf coast. Completed by Russia's Rosatom, reached full power in 2012. Spent fuel returned to Russia under bilateral agreements. Proximity to Gulf makes it strategically significant.

name: Parchin Military Complex
hardening: Dispersed site with hardened bunkers across ~60 sq km
description: Massive military-industrial complex 30 km southeast of Tehran. Iran's primary conventional weapons development and munitions manufacturing center. Subject of intense IAEA scrutiny over alleged high-explosive tests relevant to nuclear weapons development.

name: Kermanshah Missile Garrison
description: IRGC Aerospace Force ballistic missile base in western Iran, ~525 km from Israel. Houses mobile TELs and short-to-medium range missiles. Used as launch site during True Promise operations and January 2020 strikes on Al Asad Air Base.

name: Bandar Abbas Naval Base
hardening: Hardened pens for fast attack craft; underground coastal missile batteries
description: Principal naval installation for both IRIN and IRGC Navy, on the northern shore of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's most strategically important maritime facility, hosting the bulk of submarine fleet, surface combatants, and fast attack craft. Command hub for anti-access/area-denial strategy in the Strait.

name: Mehrabad Air Base
operators: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force (IRIAF)
description: Dual-use military-civilian airport in western Tehran. IRIAF 1st TFB hosts limited operational F-14A Tomcats and Iran's strategic transport fleet. Critical node for command-and-control and logistics, though highly vulnerable to precision strikes.

name: Khatami Air Base (8th TFB)
operators: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force (IRIAF)
description: Major IRIAF installation near Isfahan operating F-4 Phantom II fighter-bombers and Su-24 Fencer strike aircraft. Proximity to Isfahan's nuclear facilities means it plays a key air defense role for Iran's nuclear infrastructure.

name: Tabriz 2nd Tactical Air Base
operators: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force (IRIAF)
description: IRIAF's northwesternmost major fighter base near Turkey/Azerbaijan borders. Home to Iran's MiG-29 Fulcrums — their most capable air-superiority fighters. Defends northwestern airspace against threats from NATO's southern flank or Israeli aircraft via Turkish/Azerbaijani airspace.
